decode_thumbnail.description: Placeholder — not yet wired to the decoder.
decode_thumbnail.title: Decode Thumbnail

extract_mattes.description: Placeholder — not yet wired to the decoder.
extract_mattes.title: Extract Mattes

zenbitmaps.encode_bmp.description: BMP encoding with bit depth selection.
zenbitmaps.encode_bmp.title: Encode Bmp

zencodecs.quality_intent.description: Format selection and quality profile for encoding (zennode node).  This node controls output format selection and quality. It supports both RIAPI querystring keys and JSON API fields, matching imageflow's established `EncoderPreset::Auto` / `EncoderPreset::Format` ergonomics.  **RIAPI**: `?qp=high&accept.webp=true&accept.avif=true` **JSON**: `{ "profile": "high", "allow_webp": true, "allow_avif": true }`  When `format` is empty (default), the pipeline auto-selects the best format from the allowed set. When `format` is set (e.g., "jpeg"), that format is used directly.  The `profile` field accepts both named presets and numeric values: - Named: lowest, low, medium_low, medium, good, high, highest, lossless - Numeric: 0-100 (mapped to codec-specific quality scales)  Convert to [`CodecIntent`] via [`to_codec_intent()`](QualityIntentNode::to_codec_intent).

allow_avif.description: Allow AVIF output. Must be explicitly enabled.
allow_avif.title: Allow Avif

allow_color_profiles.description: Allow non-sRGB color profiles in the output.
allow_color_profiles.title: Allow Color Profiles

allow_jxl.description: Allow JPEG XL output. Must be explicitly enabled.
allow_jxl.title: Allow Jxl

allow_webp.description: Allow WebP output. Must be explicitly enabled.
allow_webp.title: Allow Webp

dpr.description: Device pixel ratio for quality adjustment.  Higher DPR screens tolerate lower quality (smaller pixels). Default 1.0 = no adjustment.
dpr.title: Dpr

format.description: Explicit output format. Empty = auto-select from allowed formats.  Values: "jpeg", "png", "webp", "gif", "avif", "jxl", "keep", or "". "keep" preserves the source format. "thumbnail" is an alias for the key.
format.title: Output Format

lossless.description: Global lossless preference. Empty = default (lossy).  Accepts "true", "false", or "keep" (match source losslessness).
lossless.title: Lossless

profile.description: Quality profile: named preset or numeric 0-100.  Named presets: "lowest", "low", "medium_low", "medium", "good", "high", "highest", "lossless". Numeric: "0" to "100" (codec-specific mapping).
profile.title: Quality Profile

quality_fallback.description: Legacy quality fallback (0-100). Used when `qp` is not set.  RIAPI: `?quality=85` — sets a numeric quality as fallback for all codecs. Prefer `qp` (quality profile) for new code.
quality_fallback.title: Quality (legacy)

zencodecs.quality_intent.title: Quality Intent Node

zenjpeg.encode.description: JPEG encoder configuration as a self-documenting pipeline node.  Schema-only definition for pipeline registry. Conversion to native zenjpeg config types happens in the bridge layer via `ParamMap`.
zenjpeg.encode.title: Encode Jpeg

zenlayout.crop.description: Crop the image to a pixel rectangle.  Specifies origin (x, y) and dimensions (w, h) in post-orientation source coordinates. For percentage-based cropping, use [`CropPercent`] or [`CropMargins`] instead.  RIAPI: `?crop=10,10,90,90` JSON: `{ "x": 10, "y": 10, "w": 80, "h": 80 }`

h.description: Height of the crop region in pixels.
h.title: Height

w.description: Width of the crop region in pixels.
w.title: Width

x.description: Left edge X coordinate in pixels.
x.title: X

y.description: Top edge Y coordinate in pixels.
y.title: Y

zenlayout.crop.title: Crop

zenlayout.crop_margins.description: Crop the image by removing percentage-based margins from each side.  Each value is a fraction of the corresponding source dimension to remove. CSS-style ordering: top, right, bottom, left.  JSON: `{ "top": 0.1, "right": 0.05, "bottom": 0.1, "left": 0.05 }`

bottom.description: Bottom margin as fraction of source height to remove.
bottom.title: Bottom

left.description: Left margin as fraction of source width to remove.
left.title: Left

right.description: Right margin as fraction of source width to remove.
right.title: Right

top.description: Top margin as fraction of source height to remove.
top.title: Top

zenlayout.crop_margins.title: Crop Margins

zenlayout.crop_percent.description: Crop the image using percentage-based coordinates.  All coordinates are fractions of source dimensions (0.0 = origin, 1.0 = full extent). For example, `x=0.1, y=0.1, w=0.8, h=0.8` removes 10% from each edge.  JSON: `{ "x": 0.1, "y": 0.1, "w": 0.8, "h": 0.8 }`

h.description: Height as fraction of source height (1.0 = full height).
h.title: Height

w.description: Width as fraction of source width (1.0 = full width).
w.title: Width

x.description: Left edge as fraction of source width (0.0 = left edge).
x.title: X

y.description: Top edge as fraction of source height (0.0 = top edge).
y.title: Y

zenlayout.crop_percent.title: Crop Percent

zenlayout.expand_canvas.description: Expand the canvas by adding padding around the image.  Adds specified pixel amounts to each side. The fill color defaults to "transparent" (premultiplied zero). Accepts CSS-style named colors or hex values.  JSON: `{ "left": 10, "top": 10, "right": 10, "bottom": 10, "color": "white" }`

color.description: Fill color for the expanded area.  Accepts "transparent", "white", "black", or hex "#RRGGBB" / "#RRGGBBAA".
color.title: Color

zenlayout.expand_canvas.title: Expand Canvas

zenlayout.flip_h.description: Flip the image horizontally (mirror left-right).  No parameters. Presence in the pipeline means flip is applied. Composes with other orientation operations into a single transform.  RIAPI: `?sflip=h`
zenlayout.flip_h.title: Flip H

zenlayout.flip_v.description: Flip the image vertically (mirror top-bottom).  No parameters. Presence in the pipeline means flip is applied. Composes with other orientation operations into a single transform.  RIAPI: `?sflip=v`
zenlayout.flip_v.title: Flip V

zenlayout.orient.description: Apply EXIF orientation correction.  Orientation values 1-8 follow the EXIF standard: 1 = identity, 2 = flip-H, 3 = rotate-180, 4 = flip-V, 5 = transpose, 6 = rotate-90, 7 = transverse, 8 = rotate-270.  RIAPI: `?autorotate=true` (uses embedded EXIF), `?srotate=90` JSON: `{ "orientation": 6 }`
zenlayout.orient.title: Orient

zenlayout.output_limits.description: Safety limits and codec alignment for output dimensions.  Constrains the final output to max/min dimension bounds and optionally aligns dimensions to codec block boundaries (e.g., MCU multiples for JPEG).  Processing order: max (scale down) → min (scale up) → align (snap). Max always wins over min if they conflict.  JSON: `{ "max_w": 4096, "max_h": 4096, "align_x": 16, "align_y": 16, "align_mode": "extend" }`

align_mode.description: How to handle alignment snapping.  - "crop" — round down, lose edge pixels - "extend" — round up, replicate edge pixels (default) - "distort" — round to nearest, slight stretch
align_mode.title: Align Mode

align_x.description: Horizontal alignment multiple in pixels. 0 = no alignment.  Output width will be snapped to the nearest multiple of this value. Common values: 2 (4:2:2), 8 (DCT block), 16 (4:2:0 MCU).
align_x.title: X Multiple

align_y.description: Vertical alignment multiple in pixels. 0 = no alignment.  Output height will be snapped to the nearest multiple of this value.
align_y.title: Y Multiple

max_h.description: Maximum output height. 0 = no limit. Scales down proportionally if exceeded.
max_h.title: Height

max_w.description: Maximum output width. 0 = no limit. Scales down proportionally if exceeded.
max_w.title: Width

min_h.description: Minimum output height. 0 = no minimum. Scales up proportionally if below.
min_h.title: Height

min_w.description: Minimum output width. 0 = no minimum. Scales up proportionally if below.
min_w.title: Width

zenlayout.output_limits.title: Output Limits

zenlayout.region.description: Viewport into the source image, unifying crop and pad.  Defines a rectangular window using edge coordinates, each expressed as a percentage of the source dimension plus a pixel offset: `resolved = source_dim * pct + px`.  - Viewport smaller than source = crop - Viewport extending beyond source = pad (filled with color) - Viewport entirely outside source = blank canvas  Coordinates are **edge-based** (left, top, right, bottom), not origin + size.  Examples: - Crop 10px from each edge: left_px=10, top_px=10, right_pct=1.0, right_px=-10, bottom_pct=1.0, bottom_px=-10 - Add 20px padding: left_px=-20, top_px=-20, right_pct=1.0, right_px=20, bottom_pct=1.0, bottom_px=20 - Center 50% of image: left_pct=0.25, top_pct=0.25, right_pct=0.75, bottom_pct=0.75

bottom_pct.description: Bottom edge: fraction of source height (1.0 = far edge).
bottom_pct.title: Percent

bottom_px.description: Bottom edge: pixel offset added after percentage.
bottom_px.title: Pixels

color.description: Fill color for areas outside the source image.  Accepts "transparent", "white", "black", or hex "#RRGGBB" / "#RRGGBBAA".
color.title: Color

left_pct.description: Left edge: fraction of source width (0.0 = origin).
left_pct.title: Percent

left_px.description: Left edge: pixel offset added after percentage.
left_px.title: Pixels

right_pct.description: Right edge: fraction of source width (1.0 = far edge).
right_pct.title: Percent

right_px.description: Right edge: pixel offset added after percentage.
right_px.title: Pixels

top_pct.description: Top edge: fraction of source height (0.0 = origin).
top_pct.title: Percent

top_px.description: Top edge: pixel offset added after percentage.
top_px.title: Pixels

zenlayout.region.title: Region Viewport

zenlayout.rotate_180.description: Rotate the image 180 degrees.  Decomposes to flip-H + flip-V (no axis swap), so it can be coalesced into the layout plan without pixel materialization.  RIAPI: `?srotate=180`
zenlayout.rotate_180.title: Rotate180

zenlayout.rotate_270.description: Rotate the image 270 degrees clockwise (90 counter-clockwise).  Swaps width and height. Coalesced with other geometry nodes so the layout planner computes correct dimensions through the full chain.  RIAPI: `?srotate=270`
zenlayout.rotate_270.title: Rotate270

zenlayout.rotate_90.description: Rotate the image 90 degrees clockwise.  Swaps width and height. Coalesced with other geometry nodes so the layout planner computes correct dimensions through the full chain. Pixel axis-swap happens at execution time.  RIAPI: `?srotate=90`
zenlayout.rotate_90.title: Rotate90

zenpipe.composite.description: Composite a foreground image onto a background at a position.  Two inputs required: - **background** (Canvas edge): the base image to draw onto - **foreground** (Input edge): the image being composited  Both inputs are auto-converted to premultiplied linear f32. Default blend mode is Porter-Duff source-over.

fg_x.description: X position of the foreground on the background canvas.
fg_x.title: X

fg_y.description: Y position of the foreground on the background canvas.
fg_y.title: Y

zenpipe.composite.title: Composite

zenpipe.crop_whitespace.description: Detect and crop uniform borders (whitespace trimming).  Materializes the upstream image, scans inward from each edge to find where pixel values diverge from the border color, then crops to the detected content bounds plus optional padding.  RIAPI: `?trim.threshold=80&trim.percentpadding=0.5` JSON: `{ "threshold": 80, "percent_padding": 0.5 }`

percent_padding.description: Padding around detected content as a percentage of content dimensions.  0.0 = tight crop, 0.5 = 0.5% padding on each side.
percent_padding.title: Padding

threshold.description: Color distance threshold (0–255).  Pixels within this distance of the border color are considered "whitespace". Lower = stricter, higher = more tolerant.
threshold.title: Threshold

zenpipe.crop_whitespace.title: Crop Whitespace

zenpipe.fill_rect.description: Fill a rectangle with a solid color.  Materializes the upstream image, draws the rectangle, then re-streams.  JSON: `{ "x1": 10, "y1": 10, "x2": 100, "y2": 100, "color": [255, 0, 0, 255] }`
zenpipe.fill_rect.title: Fill Rect

zenpipe.overlay.description: Overlay a small image (watermark, logo) at absolute coordinates.  Single input (the background). The overlay image data is provided via io_id (loaded from a separate input buffer).  Overlay is auto-converted to premultiplied linear f32. Opacity scales the overlay's alpha channel before compositing.

io_id.description: I/O ID of the overlay image source.
io_id.title: Overlay IO ID

x.description: X position on the background canvas.
x.title: X

y.description: Y position on the background canvas.
y.title: Y

zenpipe.overlay.title: Overlay

zenpipe.remove_alpha.description: Remove alpha channel by compositing onto a solid matte color.  Produces RGB output suitable for JPEG encoding. The compositing is done in sRGB space (matching browser behavior for CSS background-color).  JSON: `{ "matte_r": 255, "matte_g": 255, "matte_b": 255 }`
zenpipe.remove_alpha.title: Remove Alpha

zenpipe.riapi.autorotate.description: Apply EXIF orientation correction via querystring
zenpipe.riapi.autorotate.title: Auto-Rotate (RIAPI)

zenpipe.riapi.crop.description: Crop image to rectangle via querystring coordinates
zenpipe.riapi.crop.title: Crop (RIAPI)

zenpipe.riapi.flip.description: Flip image horizontally and/or vertically via querystring
zenpipe.riapi.flip.title: Flip (RIAPI)

zenpipe.riapi.frame.description: Select a specific frame from animated/multi-page images
zenpipe.riapi.frame.title: Frame Select (RIAPI)

zenpipe.riapi.rotate.description: Rotate image by 90/180/270 degrees via querystring
zenpipe.riapi.rotate.title: Rotate (RIAPI)

zenpipe.round_corners.description: Apply rounded corners with anti-aliased masking.  Generates a `RoundedRectMask` (via zenblend) and applies it to the alpha channel. Transparent corners reveal the background color, or remain transparent for PNG/WebP/AVIF output.  Supports uniform radius, per-corner radii, percentage-based radii, and circle mode (elliptical crop for non-square images).  JSON: `{ "radius": 20.0, "bg_color": [0, 0, 0, 0] }`

bg_a.description: Background color alpha channel. 0 = transparent (preserve alpha).
bg_a.title: Bg A

bg_r.description: Background color red channel (for compositing transparent corners).
bg_r.title: Bg R

radius.description: Corner radius in pixels (uniform). Clamped to min(width, height) / 2. Used when mode is "pixels" (default) or as fallback.  RIAPI: `?s.roundcorners=20` (single value) or `?s.roundcorners=10,20,30,40` (TL,TR,BR,BL)
radius.title: Radius

zenpipe.round_corners.title: Round Corners

zenpng.encode.description: PNG encoding with quality, lossless mode, and compression options.
zenpng.encode.title: Encode Png

zenresize.constrain.description: Constrain image dimensions with resize, crop, or pad modes.  The unified resize/layout node — combines layout geometry with resampling hints in a single node, matching imageflow's ergonomic Constrain API.  Optional parameters (`Option<T>`) use `None` for "not specified" — the engine picks sensible defaults based on the operation.  # Gravity  Two ways to specify gravity: - **Named anchor** (`gravity`): "center", "top_left", "bottom_right", etc. - **Percentage** (`gravity_x`/`gravity_y`): 0.0–1.0, overrides named anchor when set.  If `gravity_x` and `gravity_y` are both set, the named `gravity` is ignored.  # JSON API  Simple (named anchor): ```json { "w": 800, "h": 600, "mode": "fit_crop", "gravity": "top_left" } ```  Precise (percentage gravity): ```json { "w": 800, "h": 600, "mode": "fit_crop", "gravity_x": 0.33, "gravity_y": 0.0, "down_filter": "lanczos", "unsharp_percent": 15.0 } ```  RIAPI: `?w=800&h=600&mode=fit_crop&anchor=top_left&down.filter=lanczos`

canvas_color.description: Fill color for exterior padding regions added around the image.  Used by pad modes (fit_pad, within_pad, pad_within) to fill the canvas area outside the image content. Does NOT affect pixels inside the image — use `matte_color` for alpha compositing.  None = transparent. Accepts "transparent", "white", "black", or hex "#RRGGBB" / "#RRGGBBAA".
canvas_color.title: Canvas Color

down_filter.description: Downscale resampling filter. None = auto (Robidoux).  31 filters available: "robidoux", "robidoux_sharp", "robidoux_fast", "lanczos", "lanczos_sharp", "lanczos2", "lanczos2_sharp", "ginseng", "ginseng_sharp", "mitchell", "catmull_rom", "cubic", "cubic_sharp", "cubic_b_spline", "hermite", "triangle", "linear", "box", "fastest", "jinc", "n_cubic", "n_cubic_sharp", etc.
down_filter.title: Downscale Filter

gravity.description: Named anchor for crop/pad positioning.  Controls which part of the image is preserved when cropping, or where the image is positioned when padding.  Values: "center", "top_left", "top", "top_right", "left", "right", "bottom_left", "bottom", "bottom_right".  Overridden by `gravity_x`/`gravity_y` when both are set.
gravity.title: Anchor

gravity_x.description: Horizontal gravity (0.0 = left, 0.5 = center, 1.0 = right).  When both `gravity_x` and `gravity_y` are set, they override the named `gravity` anchor. Use for precise positioning beyond the 9 cardinal points (e.g., rule-of-thirds at 0.33).
gravity_x.title: Gravity X

gravity_y.description: Vertical gravity (0.0 = top, 0.5 = center, 1.0 = bottom).  When both `gravity_x` and `gravity_y` are set, they override the named `gravity` anchor.
gravity_y.title: Gravity Y

h.description: Target height in pixels. None = unconstrained (derive from width + aspect ratio).  RIAPI: `?h=600` or `?height=600` or `?maxheight=600` (legacy, implies mode=within).
h.title: Height

kernel_lobe_ratio.description: Negative-lobe ratio for kernel sharpening (zero-cost).  Adjusts the resampling kernel's negative lobes during weight computation. 0.0 = flatten (maximum smoothness), above filter's natural ratio = sharpen. Zero additional cost — changes the filter shape, not a separate processing step.  For post-resize unsharp mask (separate pass), use `unsharp_percent`.
kernel_lobe_ratio.title: Lobe Ratio

kernel_width_scale.description: Kernel width scale factor (zero-cost).  Multiplies the resampling kernel window width. >1.0 = softer (wider window, less aliasing), <1.0 = sharper (narrower, aliasing risk). Combined with blur. Zero additional cost.
kernel_width_scale.title: Width Scale

matte_color.description: Background color for alpha compositing (matte behind transparent pixels).  Applied during resize to prevent halo artifacts at transparent edges. Separate from `canvas_color` which fills exterior padding regions. When set, transparent pixels are composited against this color before the resampling kernel samples them.  None = no matte (preserve transparency). "white" is common for JPEG output.
matte_color.title: Matte Color

mode.description: Constraint mode controlling how the image fits the target dimensions.  - "distort" / "stretch" — stretch to exact dimensions, ignoring aspect ratio - "within" / "max" — fit inside target, never upscale (default) - "fit" — fit inside target, may upscale - "within_crop" / "crop" — fill target by cropping, never upscale - "fit_crop" — fill target by cropping, may upscale - "fit_pad" / "pad" — fit inside target, pad to exact dimensions - "within_pad" — fit inside target without upscale, pad to exact dimensions - "pad_within" — never upscale, always pad to exact canvas - "aspect_crop" / "aspectcrop" — crop to target aspect ratio without resizing - "larger_than" — upscale if needed to meet target, never downscale
mode.title: Mode

post_blur.description: Post-resize Gaussian blur sigma (real cost).  Applied as a separable H+V pass after resize. NOT equivalent to `kernel_width_scale` (which changes the kernel itself at zero cost).
post_blur.title: Gaussian Blur

resample_when.description: When to apply resampling.  - "size_differs" — only resample when dimensions change (default) - "size_differs_or_sharpening_requested" — resample when dimensions change or when sharpening is requested (allows sharpening without resize) - "always" — always resample, even at identity dimensions
resample_when.title: Resample When

scale.description: Scale control: when to allow scaling.  - "down" / "downscaleonly" — only downscale, never upscale - "up" / "upscaleonly" — only upscale, never downscale - "both" — allow both (default) - "canvas" / "upscalecanvas" — upscale canvas (pad) only  RIAPI: `?scale=down`
scale.title: Scale

scaling_colorspace.description: Color space for resampling math. None = auto ("linear" for most operations).  - "linear" — resize in linear light (gamma-correct, default) - "srgb" — resize in sRGB gamma space (faster, less correct)  RIAPI: `?down.colorspace=linear` or `?up.colorspace=srgb`
scaling_colorspace.title: Scaling Colorspace

sharpen_when.description: When to apply sharpening.  - "downscaling" — sharpen only when downscaling (default) - "upscaling" — sharpen only when upscaling - "size_differs" — sharpen whenever dimensions change - "always" — always sharpen, even at identity dimensions
sharpen_when.title: Sharpen When

unsharp_percent.description: Post-resize unsharp mask strength (0 = none, 100 = maximum).  Applied as a separate pass AFTER resampling. Adds real computational cost proportional to output dimensions. For zero-cost sharpening that adjusts the resampling kernel itself, use `kernel_lobe_ratio`.  None = no unsharp mask.
unsharp_percent.title: Unsharp Mask

up_filter.description: Upscale resampling filter. None = auto (Ginseng).  Same filter names as down_filter.
up_filter.title: Upscale Filter

w.description: Target width in pixels. None = unconstrained (derive from height + aspect ratio).  RIAPI: `?w=800` or `?width=800` or `?maxwidth=800` (legacy, implies mode=within).
w.title: Width

zoom.description: Device pixel ratio / zoom multiplier.  Multiplies target dimensions. 2.0 means w and h are doubled. RIAPI: `?zoom=2`, `?dpr=2x`, `?dppx=1.5`
zoom.title: DPR / Zoom

zenresize.constrain.title: Constrain

zenresize.resize.description: Forced resize to exact dimensions (no layout planning).  Unlike [`Constrain`] which applies constraint modes (fit, crop, pad), this resizes unconditionally to the specified width × height. Used when the caller has already determined the target dimensions.  Skipped at compile time when input dimensions match target (identity resize).  JSON: `{ "w": 400, "h": 300, "filter": "robidoux" }` RIAPI: Not directly exposed — use Constrain for querystring-driven resize.

filter.description: Resampling filter name (e.g., "robidoux", "lanczos", "mitchell"). Empty string = default (Robidoux).
filter.title: Filter

zenresize.resize.title: Resize

zentiff.encode.description: TIFF encoding with compression and predictor options.
zentiff.encode.title: Encode Tiff

method.description: VP8L compression method (0 = fastest, 6 = best). Separate from effort — method controls the algorithm tier, effort controls quality within that tier.
method.title: Method
